ādiyati take.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply ādiyati, “takes up, grasps”.

ādiyeyyāmā.meaning: we should attend to; we should take up
ādiyeyyāmā.gram: verb, 1st person plural optative of ādiyati (√ādā)
breakdown: Root: to take; ādiyati: to take up, undertake.
1st person plural optative ending: “we should” (with metrically long final -ā).

ādiyissati.meaning: will attend to, will seize, will take up
ādiyissati.gram: verb, 3rd person singular future of ādiyati (√ādā)
breakdown: Root: to take; ādiyati: to take up.
Here from √dā “to take”: the act of taking — grasping, holding.

ādānapaṭinissagge.meaning: in the giving up of grasping; in the renouncing of clinging

ādāso.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (ā + √dis, to see)
breakdown: Root: to see. ādāsa: that in which one sees oneself — a mirror.

āgacchati.meaning: comes to, approaches; arrives, comes back
breakdown: Root: to go. ā + √gam: to come.

āgama.meaning: coming, approach; result; (also) received tradition, scripture
breakdown: Root: to go. āgama: the coming, what has come down.
Action-noun suffix: the act of coming.

āgameti.meaning: causes someone to come to one; waits, awaits
āgameti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of āgacchati
breakdown: Root: to go. Causative āgame: to make come — to wait for.

āgamma.meaning: owing to, by means of; depending on
āgamma.gram: gerund of āgacchati used adverbially (ā + √gam + ya)
breakdown: Gerund ending: literally “having come to” — hence owing to, by means of.

āgantar.meaning: one who is coming or going to come

āgantu.meaning: occasional, incidental; adventitious, coming from outside
breakdown: Root: to go. āgantu: what comes in from outside — incidental.

āgantuka.meaning: coming, arriving; a newcomer, guest, stranger

āgataṭṭhāne.meaning: in the place arrived at; in the state or condition attained

āgati.gram: feminine abstract noun, from āgacchati (ā + √gam + ti)

āgato.gram: past participle of āgacchati, masculine nominative singular

āghātavatthusuttaṁ.meaning: the Āghātavatthu Sutta, the “Discourse on the Grounds of Hatred”
āghātavatthusuttaṁ.gram: proper noun (title of a sutta); neuter compound

āghātavatthūni.meaning: the grounds of hatred; the occasions for resentment

āgilāyati be.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply āgilāyati, “to be tired, to ache”.
breakdown: Is tired, aches (ā + √gilā, to be weary).

āgāmin.meaning: returning; one who returns (to this world)
breakdown: Possessive/agent suffix: one who comes back.

āgāḷhenapi.meaning: even with what is harsh; even by rough (speech)
āgāḷhenapi.gram: past participle/adjective, instrumental singular + enclitic pi

āha.gram: verb, perfect of √ah (to say), 3rd person singular

āhacca.meaning: having struck; striking, beating, throwing against

āhananapariyāhananaraso.meaning: having the function of striking and striking all around; whose essence is impact (a commentarial definition of the earth element's hardness or of contact)
breakdown: Striking all around, heavy striking (pari + āhanana).
Function, essence, taste (in commentarial usage: characteristic function).

āhanaṁ.meaning: striking; I strike (in verse: beating the drum of the deathless)
āhanaṁ.gram: verb form of āhanati (ā + √han), 1st person singular present/participle in verse
āhanaṁ.note: divergence: the lexicon line here glosses a different cluster (ucchādana, parimaddana, nahāpana — anointing, massage, bathing); āhanaṁ itself is from āhanati, “to strike”.

breakdown: Root: to carry. ā + √har: to bring.
Root: to offer, sacrifice. āhuna: an oblation.

āhuneyyo.meaning: worthy of gifts, worthy of adoration (an epithet of the Saṅgha)
breakdown: Suffix of worthiness: worthy of receiving offerings.

āhuneyyo worthy of.meaning: āhuneyyo: worthy of gifts, of adoration
āhuneyyo worthy of.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply āhuneyyo, “worthy of gifts”.

āhuneyyā.gram: adjective, masculine nominative plural of āhuneyya
breakdown: Suffix of worthiness: worthy of receiving offerings.

āhuneyyā worthy of.summary: A glossary fragment; the entry is simply āhuneyyā, “worthy of offering”.

ājānāhi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular imperative of ājānāti (+ particle hi as the lexicon parses)
breakdown: Root: to know. ājānāti: to understand, perceive.
2nd person singular imperative ending (the lexicon also reads an enclitic hi, “because”).

ājīvahetupaññattānaṁ.meaning: of (rules) made known for the sake of livelihood; of what has been declared on account of livelihood
breakdown: Made known, declared, laid down (past participle of paññāpeti).

ājīvapārisuddhisīlaṁ.meaning: the virtue of purity of livelihood; sīla consisting in purification of one's means of living

breakdown: Root: to live. ājīva: livelihood — as in sammā-ājīvo, right livelihood.

ākaṅkhamānā.gram: present participle of ākaṅkhati, feminine singular or masculine plural

ākoṭeyya.meaning: would beat, would thrash; should knock
ākoṭeyya.gram: verb, 3rd person singular optative of ākoṭeti (ā + √kuṭ)
breakdown: Root: to strike. ākoṭeti: to beat, knock.

ākāraparivitakkena.meaning: by careful consideration of reasons; through examining the grounds
breakdown: Careful consideration, examination (pari + vitakka, thorough thinking).

ālayarāmā.meaning: delighting in clinging; taking pleasure in attachment (said of beings)
breakdown: Clinging, attachment; abode, what one hangs on to.

āmakadhaññapaṭiggahaṇā.meaning: from accepting raw grain (one of the abstentions in the monk's morality)
āmakadhaññapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter compound action noun, ablative singular

āmakamaṁsapaṭiggahaṇā.meaning: from accepting raw meat (one of the abstentions in the monk's morality)
āmakamaṁsapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter compound action noun, ablative singular

āmantayati, āmanteti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present (ā + √mant); two stem forms
breakdown: Root: to address, consult. āmanteti/āmantayati: to address, call, invite.

āmisakiñcikkhahetu.meaning: for the sake of some little material gain; on account of a trifling reward
breakdown: Material gain, reward; food, the carnal.
For the sake of, by reason of.

āmisantaro.meaning: having gain within; inwardly intent on reward, wishing for gain
breakdown: Inner, within. Together: one whose inner motive is gain.
